content_of_post_to_comment: Some family members will travel miles to attend your funeral, yet won’t cross the street to support you while you’re alive. Mine wouldn’t do either. Which, strangely enough, taught me something. Love isn’t measured by who mourns you publicly. It’s measured by who shows up while you’re still here.
comment: There’s an interesting tension here: sometimes people grieve the role someone played in their life more than the relationship they actually maintained. Public mourning can reveal attachment, regret, or guilt—not always the love we needed when it mattered.
